q: The study of interference and diffraction deals with _______ optics.
A _______ is a surface of constant phase.
The secondary wavelets have the same _______ as the primary wavelets.
In Young’s double-slit experiment, the central fringe is always _______.
The distance between two consecutive bright fringes is called _______.
Constructive interference occurs when the path difference is an _______ multiple of the wavelength.
Destructive interference occurs when the path difference is an _______ multiple of λ/2.
The phenomenon of bending of light around the corners of an obstacle is called _______.
A diffraction _______ consists of a large number of equally spaced parallel slits.
Two sources are said to be _______ if they emit waves of the same frequency and have a constant phase difference.
The ray approximation of geometrical optics is not valid for the _______ of light.
The _______ angle is one of the important characteristics of the periodic wave.
If the phase difference is 0, 2π, 4π, ..., the two positions are in _______.
The secondary wavelets spread out in all directions at the same _______ as the primary wavelet.
For diffraction to be significant, the size of the slit must be _______ to the wavelength of light.
The pattern of bright and dark _______ is called an interference pattern.
Interference in _______ films is responsible for the colors in soap bubbles.
The condition for the m-th order maximum in a grating is d sin θ = _______.
In a single-slit diffraction pattern, the _______ maximum is the brightest.
Light waves are _______ electromagnetic waves.
The distance between the slits in Young’s experiment is denoted by _______.
The fringe width β is given by the formula β = _______.
When light travels from one medium to another, its _______ remains constant.
Newton’s _______ are formed by interference between light waves reflected from the upper and lower surfaces of the air gap.
The _______ approximation is valid when the wavelength is very small compared to the opening.
A _______ wavefront is produced by a source at a very large distance.
Huygens's _______ states that every point on a wavefront is a source of secondary wavelets.
The _______ of light is determined by its frequency.
Physical optics is also known as _______ optics.
In interference, the total _______ is conserved but redistributed.
The central maximum of a diffraction pattern becomes _______ as the slit width decreases.
The _______ power of an optical instrument is limited by diffraction.
A grating with N lines per metre has a grating _______ d = 1/N.
Monochromatic light consists of waves of a _______ wavelength.
The path difference for the first dark fringe in a single-slit diffraction is _______.
Interference and diffraction are _______ properties of waves.
The intensity of light is proportional to the _______ of the amplitude.
In Young’s experiment, if the source is white light, the central fringe is _______.
The first order maximum occurs when m = _______.
The _______ of the fringes depends on the wavelength of the light used.
An _______ film on water shows different colors due to interference.
Wave optics can explain the _______ of light through small openings.
The distance from the slits to the screen is denoted by _______.
The _______ difference is related to the path difference by (2π/λ) × path difference.
A _______ source is required to observe a stable interference pattern.
The diffraction pattern of a _______ opening is a series of concentric rings.
If the path difference is (m + 1/2)λ, _______ interference occurs.
The _______ of light in a medium is less than its speed in a vacuum.
Diffraction _______ are much sharper and more intense than interference fringes.
Wave optics is a more _______ theory than geometrical optics.
